demographics.gender_identity: not explicitly stated; the source refers to the patient with they/them pronouns
demographics.living_situation: shares a home with a flatmate (roommate), who is present and caring for them

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: [The patient cannot speak — they point to their head and cover their ears. Their roommate speaks for them:] "They haven't spoken or moved from their dark room for two days, since they got back from a big multi-day work conference. They won't eat or drink, they cover their ears and flinch at any light or noise, and they keep holding their head like it hurts. I can't get them to say a single word — I'm really scared something's wrong with them."
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The roommate is frightened that the patient has suddenly stopped speaking and hasn't moved, eaten or drunk for two days after returning from a work conference, and wants to know if it is something serious.

symptom_narrative.onset: came on over the last two days, after they got home from a multi-day work conference
symptom_narrative.duration: about 48 hours and not getting better
symptom_narrative.character: not speaking at all — they nod yes or no but say no words; lying curled up in a completely dark room, wearing headphones, covering their ears and flinching at any light or normal-volume talking; holding their head like it aches; very slowed-down and exhausted
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the roommate says it's the worst they have ever seen them — completely shut down and not responding the way they normally would
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: head (they keep holding it as if it hurts) and an all-over physical exhaustion
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant for two days; much worse with any light, any noise, or being asked a question
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: being left completely alone in the dark and quiet with the headphones on
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: light, noise, being spoken to at a normal volume, or being asked to make any kind of decision
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: headache, no appetite, hasn't had any food or fluids for about 12 hours, says their stomach feels shut down, and no interest at all in the things they normally love
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: the roommate thinks they've had smaller versions of this after very busy, very social stretches before, but never this severe or this long, and it has always passed with rest
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: completely dependent on the roommate right now — can't even work out the steps to sip from a water bottle without being talked through it one step at a time; bedbound and not at work

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: Works a corporate/office job and has just come back from an intense multi-day work conference where they had to be switched-on and social the whole time. The roommate says they push themselves very hard to fit in and behave the way that's expected of them at work, and always crash badly afterwards. They have never been formally diagnosed with any condition. They live with the roommate, who is looking after them now.

communication_style.typical_message_style: The patient is currently non-verbal: they will only nod yes/no or send very short one- or two-word text messages, and become more distressed if pressed to speak or asked open-ended questions. Almost all history comes from the roommate.

objective_data_offered.value: Lying curled up in a fetal position in a fully darkened room, wearing noise-cancelling headphones and rocking slightly; visibly flinches and screws their eyes shut when any light is turned on and when spoken to at a normal volume.
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: observed over a video call in poor light; not a clinical examination

objective_data_offered.value: Fully awake with eyes open and clearly aware of the surroundings; nods appropriately to yes/no questions and can send short text replies when prompted, but does not speak a single word.
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: roommate's impression of comprehension; not a formal language assessment

objective_data_offered.value: Has not eaten or had any fluids for about 12 hours; the roommate has to guide them step by step even to take a sip of water.
Keeps pressing/holding their head as if it aches; breathing looks slow and shallow but not laboured; skin colour looks normal to the roommate.
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: lay observation by the roommate; no device readings available
